text: Risk management is essential in any project or business. It begins with identifying risks, which are potential problems that could cause delays or financial loss. Once identified, you develop mitigation strategies to reduce the likelihood or impact of these risks. For example, you might train staff or improve safety procedures. However, not all risks can be avoided, so you also prepare contingency plans. These are backup actions to take if a risk actually occurs, such as using a different supplier. Finally, you must monitor risks regularly, because new threats can appear, and existing plans may become outdated. Effective risk management helps organisations stay prepared and resilient.
